channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UNSC Prepares to Vote on Strait of Hormuz Security Resolution
item.description: The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is scheduled to vote on a resolution concerning the Strait of Hormuz, drafted by Bahrain in collaboration with the Gulf Cooperation Council and Jordan. This ...

item.title: PTI and JUI-F leaders press for multi‑party response to Middle East war and fuel price hikes
item.description: Jailed senior leaders of Pakistan Tehreek‑i‑Insaf (PTI) have called on their party to immediately convene a multi‑party conference of like‑minded parties under the platform of Tehreek‑i‑Tahafuz Ayeen‑...

item.title: Why Admitting 'I Don’t Know' Makes Better Doctors
item.description: In this reflective piece, Dr Ranjana Srivastava discusses the challenges and responsibilities doctors face in a world of medical uncertainty. Drawing from her experience in oncology, she explains that...

item.title: Nine Arrested in Las Vegas Dice Game Shooting Resulting from Gang Dispute
item.description: A fatal shooting in Las Vegas has resulted in the death of Michael Simmons Jr., 22, who was reportedly not the intended target. The incident occurred during a dice game at a Coja Street residence on 9...

item.title: E-bike and e-scooter fire incidents reach alarming record in the UK
item.description: In 2022, the number of e-bike and e-scooter fires in the UK reached record highs, with 432 incidents involving e-bikes and 147 involving e-scooters. The rise in fires has been attributed to faulty bat...

item.title: Minimalist Mirrored Home Integrates Seamlessly with Mexican Forest
item.description: The innovative Los Terrenos mirrored home in Mexico presents a striking example of modern architecture that blends harmoniously with its forest surroundings. Unlike conventional glass houses, this des...

item.title: JPMorgan CEO warns of mounting risks to global markets amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has highlighted a series of escalating risks that could challenge the resilience of global markets. From geopolitical tensions, particularly the situation in the Middle East, ...

item.title: Seven Eritrean footballers unaccounted for in South Africa following AFCON qualifier
item.description: After their historic 2–1 away win over Eswatini in the Africa Cup of Nations qualifier, seven players from the Eritrea national football team have reportedly not returned home. Sources close to the te...

item.title: Commercial Operations at Gwadar Port Strengthen with Arrival of Malaysian Cargo Ship
item.description: Gwadar Port has witnessed a notable revival in its commercial activities as the Malaysian cargo ship MV Riva Glory docked at the port carrying 14,629 metric tonnes of transhipment cargo. This shipment...

item.title: Astronomers Map Chemical History of Spiral Galaxy NGC 1365 Using Extragalactic Archaeology
item.description: Astronomers have applied a novel method called extragalactic archaeology to study the chemical evolution of the spiral galaxy NGC 1365. This technique, previously used only within the Milky Way, exami...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal wedding called off after groom and father arrested for drunk driving
item.description: A wedding in Newcastle, KwaZulu-Natal, was unexpectedly cancelled after both the groom and his father were arrested for drunk driving. The groom had been stopped and taken for a blood alcohol test jus...

item.title: Bwala Clarifies He Never Called Tinubu a Drug Lord, Criticises Misinformation
item.description: Daniel Bwala, Special Adviser to the President on Policy Communication and Media, don clarify say he no ever call Bola Tinubu 'drug lord'. The matter wey dey circulate online and even for some newspap...

item.title: Samsung Discontinues Its Messages App in July 2026, Directs Users to Google Messages
item.description: Samsung has announced it will discontinue its proprietary Samsung Messages app in July 2026, urging Galaxy device users to switch to Google Messages as the default messaging application for a more con...

item.title: Artemis II Crew Reaches Record Lunar Distance as Global Political Tensions Rise
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission has successfully achieved the closest lunar approach by astronauts in over fifty years. The crew came within approximately 4,067 miles of the Moon, setting a new record for t...

item.title: Police arrest more than 1,000 people in Limpopo as part of Easter crime crackdown
item.description: More than 1,000 individuals were taken into custody across Limpopo province during a weeklong police operation over the Easter holidays. The South African Police Service (SAPS) reported that 1,023 sus...

item.title: Why Some People Struggle to Text Back and How It Affects Their Relationships
item.description: Some people face an inexplicable barrier when it comes to texting back, which can significantly impact their relationships. While many people may find it easy to quickly reply, others experience feeli...

item.title: Arteta disappointed by Arsenal's FA Cup exit to Southampton
item.description: Mikel Arteta expressed his disappointment after Arsenal were knocked out of the FA Cup by Championship side Southampton, with a 2-1 loss in the quarterfinals. The defeat followed Arsenal's recent loss...

item.title: Warsaw’s Neon Museum Revives Interest in Cold War-Era Neon Signs
item.description: In Warsaw, neon signs that once symbolised light and hope during the Cold War are being rediscovered and preserved. After communism ended in the late 1980s, many of these iconic signs were abandoned, ...

item.title: Protest by Diamer-Bhasha Dam Affected People Blocks Karakoram Highway for Days
item.description: A protest by people affected by the Diamer-Bhasha Dam project has brought the Karakoram Highway (KKH) to a standstill for several days, severely disrupting the land connection between Gilgit-Baltistan...

item.title: Global markets show modest gains as oil rises amid tensions with Iran
item.description: Global financial markets showed cautious optimism on Tuesday, with US and Australian shares edging higher ahead of a critical deadline set by President Donald Trump to target Iranian power plants. The...
